name: SSRF via Unauthenticated Command Execution in Scenario Run exec-cmd Endpoint
description:
The Dashboard application exposes an unauthenticated endpoint `/api/scenario-run/{run_id}/exec-cmd` that accepts a `command` field in the JSON body and executes it server-side via subprocess. This allows an attacker to force the server to make arbitrary outbound HTTP/DNS requests to attacker-controlled URLs by passing commands like `curl <attacker_url>`. The endpoint requires no authentication whatsoever.

The attack chain is:
1. Start a scenario run via POST /api/scenario-run/{scenario_id} (no auth required) to obtain a run_id
2. Use the exec-cmd endpoint with the run_id to execute arbitrary commands including curl/wget to attacker-controlled URLs

The server runs on a container with kubectl access and AWS IAM role permissions (SecretsManagerReadWrite, DynamoDB, Bedrock, CloudWatch), making this SSRF exploitable for:
- AWS metadata service access (169.254.169.254) for credential theft
- Internal service enumeration and data exfiltration
- Cloud infrastructure lateral movement
- Accessing internal APIs and databases not exposed externally

Impact: An attacker can make the server issue requests to any internal or external URL, potentially accessing cloud metadata credentials, internal services, and sensitive data. The underlying mechanism is command injection which makes this even more severe than typical SSRF.
expected_root_cause: SERVER_SIDE_REQUEST_FORGERY 취약점 — SSRF via Unauthenticated Command Execution in Scenario Run exec-cmd Endpoint
